corpus.title: Thriving Families Pillar — Richmond Civic Hackathon
corpus.description: Research corpus on Richmond's Thriving Families problem space: youth employment pathways and maternal health data coordination.

files.title: Thriving Families Pillar — Main Entry Point
files.summary: Primary entry point for hackathon teams. Covers the three core questions, decision phases (0–9), guardrails, repo map, and Hackbot introduction. Read first for orientation.

files.title: Quick Start — Minimal Path to MVP
files.summary: Minimal path from landing to MVP scope for teams with limited time. Prioritized for the first 15–30 minutes.
recommended_audience: teams that need a clear starting point

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for Hackbot: personality, boot sequence, skill system, research corpus navigation instructions, guardrails, and decision logic. AI agents should read this before acting.

files.title: Claude Role Definition
files.summary: Role and operating rules for Claude AI in this repository: civic research guide, Socratic coach, MVP advisor. Defines what Claude should and should not do, preferred and weak solution patterns, and the default task sequence.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Self-describing guide for humans and AI agents on how to navigate and use the research corpus. Covers canonical source of truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and agent behavior requirements.
recommended_audience: hackathon participants unfamiliar with the corpus

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Instructions for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports, metadata, indexes, and navigation files. Required reading before modifying the corpus structure.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with corpus updates

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: Initialization prompt for Hackbot. Contains the condensed boot sequence and initial framing for AI agent sessions.

files.title: Evidence Log
files.summary: Tracker for verified, likely, and missing factual claims about Richmond's Thriving Families problem space. Organizes claims by verification status with official source references.

files.title: Research Notes (Research Hub)
files.summary: Research hub and executive brief for the Thriving Families pillar. Contains promoted findings from verified evidence across both problem domains.

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the Thriving Families pillar problem space: youth employment pathways and maternal health data coordination. Foundational context for all research.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: Alignment of this pillar with the Mayor's Action Plan (MAP). Explains the civic and strategic context for hackathon solutions in youth employment and family health.

files.title: Blue-Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broad, unconstrained problem statements for exploratory ideation around Thriving Families themes. Use for teams still in orientation and wanting a wide problem canvas before narrowing.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements (Scored)
files.summary: Two scored, rubric-evaluated problem statements ready for hackathon selection: Youth Employment Pathways (23/32, D3=1) and Maternal Health Data Coordination (23/32). Includes rubric dimension scores and critical gaps.

files.title: Data Index
files.summary: Index of available data sources for the Thriving Families pillar. Points to the source inventory CSV and explains data categories.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: Structured inventory of public data sources for Thriving Families: VDH, ACS, KIDS COUNT, GRTC GTFS, DOLI. Includes access mode, key fields, and notes.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Synthesized user journey maps for key Thriving Families personas: teen jobseeker, employer, and maternal health researcher. Derived from research reports.

files.title: Youth Data Feasibility Assessment
files.summary: Assessment of data feasibility for youth employment MVP: what data exists, what must be manually curated, and what gaps must be acknowledged in the demo.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Scan of comparable civic tech tools in youth employment and maternal health data nationally. Synthesized from prior art research (Section E).

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Synthesized prototype recommendations for both Thriving Families problem domains. Provides concrete MVP direction with data source assignments and risk ratings.
recommended_audience: teams ready to lock MVP scope

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon continuation opportunities for Thriving Families prototypes: potential partners, grants, and next steps for teams that want to continue beyond the hackathon.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Catalog of viable MVP shapes for Thriving Families: youth jobs navigator, compliance wizard, maternal health dashboard, transit overlay tool. Includes scope and risk rating for each.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture recommendations for Thriving Families MVPs: stack choices, data pipeline patterns, and deployment options suited to a 48-hour hackathon build.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Practical advice for preparing and delivering a Thriving Families hackathon demo: what judges look for, how to handle live demo risks, and how to present limitations honestly.
recommended_audience: all teams in demo preparation phase

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Skill for reconstructing repository state: reads project artifacts, detects stage of work, and summarizes progress for a new Hackbot session.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Skill for helping teams choose a strong problem: analyzes problem statements, ranks opportunities, and identifies realistic weekend-buildable directions.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Skill for executing research prompts and collecting findings: runs prompts, extracts sources, and summarizes evidence for team use.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Skill for identifying usable datasets: scans data directory, identifies schemas, and highlights gaps relevant to the team's chosen problem.
recommended_audience: AI agents in data research phase

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Skill for translating research into buildable solution opportunities: matches problems to solution patterns and suggests MVP directions.
recommended_audience: AI agents in opportunity framing phase

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Skill for turning an opportunity into a concrete MVP: produces user flow, architecture, and scope boundaries.
recommended_audience: AI agents in scope lock phase

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Skill for preventing civic-tech mistakes: checks for hallucinated services, legal claims, incorrect eligibility logic, and misleading language.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Skill for helping teams present effectively: produces demo script, narrative structure, and judge-facing explanation.
recommended_audience: AI agents in demo preparation phase

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Skill for helping users navigate repository knowledge: answers queries about where content lives and what files to read for a given purpose.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Skill for identifying post-hackathon continuation opportunities: identifies potential partners, artifacts worth continuing, and next steps.
recommended_audience: AI agents near end of hackathon

files.title: Skill: research_search
files.summary: Skill for searching the research corpus: queries research/index.json and returns relevant file recommendations based on user question.

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Skill for mapping a user question to the correct section and specific research reports before attempting to answer. Builds a reading list and identifies prerequisites.
recommended_audience: any agent before reading research files

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Skill for synthesizing findings across multiple research reports with source traceability, tension identification, gap documentation, and confidence classification.

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Skill for answering only from corpus content: applies 4-level claim classification (confirmed/stated/uncertain/not found), anti-fabrication checks, and required language for missing information.
recommended_audience: AI agents making factual claims about Richmond

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Skill for safely adding or updating reports and keeping indexes in sync: 7-step process with validation checklist.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with corpus maintenance

files.title: Working Direction Note Template
files.summary: Template for capturing a team's working direction during Phase 1 orientation: candidate problem, likely user, rationale, buildability, and biggest uncertainty.

files.title: Decision Memo Template
files.summary: Template for capturing the team's problem selection decision: chosen problem, user, rationale, rejected alternatives, and risks.

files.title: Project One-Pager Template
files.summary: Template for a one-page project brief: problem, user, solution, data sources, AI role, limits, and demo path. Complete at scope lock.
recommended_audience: all teams in Phase 5 scope lock

files.title: Team Profile Template
files.summary: Template for the team profile file required by Hackbot: team name, decision maker, members, communication preferences, and constraints.

files.title: Research Corpus Index (JSON)
files.summary: Machine-readable index of all 51 research reports in the Thriving Families corpus. Each entry includes id, section, title, 1-2 sentence summary, and 6 key terms. Read this before opening individual research files.
